title: Oseon 6845
content:

{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''[…]'' at the moment, we are gentlebeings of leisure. We have a hundred seventy-three-odd thousand credits I won on Oseon 6845, after all.|Lando Calrissian, to Vuffi Raa|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
'''Oseon 6845''' was the largest [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] in the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]]. A 700-[[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]]-wide [[Iron/Legends|iron]]-and-rock [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]], it was [[Atmosphere/Legends|airless]] and had extremely weak [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]; however, the inhabitants of Oseon 6845 locally augmented the latter and increased its rotation speed. As a result, landing [[Starship/Legends|starships]] on the asteroid was difficult, leading to a [[North polar spaceport|spaceport]] being constructed at its northern pole.

The asteroid was honeycombed with luxurious [[Hotel/Legends|hotels]], [[Standard day/Legends|night]] entertainment venues, and stately residences. It attracted affluent individuals from throughout the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] who engaged in shopping and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] in both Oseon 6845's underground establishments and on the [[Esplanade]], an equator-stretching pedestrian thoroughfare. For three [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] each [[Local year|year]], the phenomenon of the Oseon [[Star system/Legends|system]] known as the [[Flamewind of Oseon/Legends|Flamewind]] made interasteroidal [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel practically impossible. As a result, individuals present on Oseon 6845 at that [[Time/Legends|time]] became effectively stranded and engaged themselves in numerous parties.

Between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|sorcerer]] [[Rokur Gepta]] arranged for [[Senior administrator/Legends|Senior Administrator]] [[Lob Doluff]] of the Oseon system to invite the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], against whom Gepta held a grudge, to play the card game [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] on Oseon 6845. One night, Calrissian was ambushed at his [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|freighter]] by a [[Kenow|member]] of the [[Renatasian Confederation]], which was targeting the gambler's [[Pilot/Legends|copilot]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]], [[Vuffi Raa]]. After Calrissian [[Death/Legends|killed]] his attacker in self-defense, he was arrested on the charge of carrying a lethal [[Stingbeam/Legends|weapon]]. In lieu of capital punishment, Doluff forced the gambler to [[Transport/Legends|transport]] two [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement]] [[Police officer/Legends|officers]] to the asteroid [[Oseon 5792]], where Gepta intended to entrap Calrissian.

==Description==
===An asteroid of the Oseon system===
{{Quote|Nonetheless, had we been making a genuine planetary reentry, into a full atmosphere and full gravity, this accident would have destroyed us, Master.|Vuffi Raa, to Lando Calrissian, on an explosion shortly before landing on Oseon 6845|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
Oseon 6845,<ref name="LCFO" /> also referred to simply as Oseon,<ref name="Underworld">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref> was the largest [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] body in the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]],<ref name="LCFO" /> which lay within the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]], a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Along with the rest of the Oseon [[Star system/Legends|system]], Oseon 6845 was connected by the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Arleen system/Legends|Arleen system]], while the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route known as the [[Cadma Conduit]] linked it to the [[Erilnar system/Legends|Erilnar system]] and the<ref name="TEA" /> [[Cadma sector/Legends|Cadma sector's]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Dagelin Minor system/Legends|Dagelin Minor]] and [[Simbarc system/Legends|Simbarc systems]].<ref name="TEA" /> A [[Constellation/Legends|constellation]] was visible from Oseon 6845 that the local inhabitants had named the [[Silly Rabbit]].<ref name="LCFO" />

[[File:Oseon-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Oseon 6845 was the largest asteroid in the Oseon system.]]
An [[Iron/Legends|iron]]-based rock [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] with a mass of a billion [[Metric ton/Legends|tons]] and a diameter of 700 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] that was also classified as a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]], Oseon 6845 was located in the [[Sixth Belt]], one of the seven broad [[Oseon asteroid belt|bands]] of [[Planet/Legends|subplanetary]] bodies that circled the [[Star/Legends|star]] system's [[Oseon sun|sun]]. It had negligible natural [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], which was augmented by its inhabitants close to the asteroid's surface and canceled altogether in the proximity of its core. The rotation period of Oseon 6845 was also artificially shortened to twenty-five [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]], which provided a familiar sense of [[Standard day/Legends|day]] and night to the inhabitants.<ref name="LCFO" />

However, the resultant speed of eighty-eight [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers an hour]] at which the asteroid's barren, rocky surface moved, coupled with the worldlet's small size, made landing [[Starship/Legends|starships]] there difficult. Oseon 6845 was naturally airless, although artificial [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] was present in inhabited areas of the asteroid, with its [[Temperature/Legends|temperature]] being considerably lower closer to its core than at its surface.<ref name="LCFO" />

===The Flamewind of Oseon===
{{Quote|As you probably are aware, it is perilous in the extreme, and also illegal, for ships to travel from asteroid to asteroid in the Oseon during Flamewind.|Administrator Senior Lob Doluff, to Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
Once per each [[local year]], Oseon 6845, along with every other orbital body in the Oseon system, was affected by a phenomenon known as the [[Flamewind of Oseon/Legends|Flamewind]]. Caused by the [[Solar flare/Legends|flares]] of the Oseon sun tearing excited vapor from the closest asteroids, the Flamewind manifested itself as fluorescent and multi-[[Color/Legends|colored]] bands of ionized [[Gas/Legends|gas]] millions of kilometers both long and wide that continuously shifted across the entire [[Spectrum/Legends|spectrum]] of visible light.<ref name="LCFO" />

Accompanying the visual display were frequent static discharges between the system's asteroids and a constant barrage of [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] and particles, such as [[Electron/Legends|electrons]]. As a result, navigational, [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]], and [[Life support/Legends|life-support]] equipment was damaged, electronic communications between the asteroids and with the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] were made impossible, and the perception and behavior of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings who were not protected by adequate shielding was affected.<ref name="LCFO" />

==History==
===Luring Calrissian===
{{Quote|To Captain Lando Calrissian of the ''Millennium Falcon'': greetings and salutations! I am Lob Doluff, Administrator Senior of the Oseon System. You haven't heard of me, I'll wager, but, my dear boy, I have heard of you! Your reputation as a player of sabacc is perhaps wider spread and more salubrious than you know. My associates and I, a small group of fanciers of the game, would like to invite you here at your convenience, to play with us. If you are interested, please name the time and stakes. Every courtesy will be extended to you during your stay with us. My very warmest and anticipatory regards to you. Lob Doluff, signing off.|Message from Lob Doluff|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
[[File:The_Mindharp_of_Sharu.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Lando Calrissian (left) prevented Rokur Gepta from obtaining the Mindharp of Sharu artifact (right), causing the latter to attempt vengeance upon the former in the Oseon system.]]
At some point between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] novel trilogy, including ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].</ref> the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|sorcerer]] [[Rokur Gepta]] wished to exact vengeance upon the [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]],<ref name="LCFO" /> who played a role in the former's failure to acquire the [[Mindharp of Sharu]] artifact in the Centrality's [[Rafa system/Legends|Rafa system]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> To that end, Gepta ensured behind the scenes that Calrissian constantly faced reduced profits from his freight-hauling operations, which effectively caused the [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] to steadily lose [[Currency/Legends|money]] in his business ventures.<ref name="LCFO" />

Finally, Gepta arranged for [[Lob Doluff]], the [[Administrator Senior]] of the Oseon system, to send a message to Calrissian on the planet [[Dilonexa XXIII]], inviting the gambler to come play the [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] card game on Oseon 6845. Faced with a dwindling supply of financial resources as well as Calrissian's general dislike for interstellar trading, the captain and his [[Pilot/Legends|copilot]], the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Vuffi Raa]], departed the [[Dilonexa system/Legends|Dilonexa system]] for the Oseon system.<ref name="LCFO" />

However, while Calrissian's [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|starship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], was still being [[Fuel/Legends|refueled]] on Dilonexa XXIII, one of Gepta's agents planted a pair of [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]] on the [[Light freighter/Legends|vessel]]. The first of the explosives detonated when the starship's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] was engaged several days before its arrival in the Oseon. The second bomb exploded upon the ''Millennium Falcon'' reverting from hyperspace to [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] near Oseon 6845, but the vessel emerged relatively unscathed due to the explosive being designed to destroy a ship entering the atmosphere and gravity of a planet as opposed to an airless asteroid. Vuffi Raa subsequently oversaw the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} setting down on the surface of Oseon 6845 in what was the inexperienced Calrissian's first starship landing under the droid's piloting tutelage.<ref name="LCFO" />

===Sabacc on 6845===
{{Quote|Captain Calrissian, I believe you'd better hurry to the spaceport. I have a message here that your ship, the ''Millennium Falcon'', is on fire.|Lob Doluff|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
[[File:Lando-FlamewindCover.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Lando Calrissian during his visit in the Oseon system]]
In his first evening in the Oseon system, Calrissian played sabacc at the exclusive [[Hotel Drofo]]. After inspecting the damage caused to the ''Millennium Falcon'' by the second explosion, Vuffi Raa visited the gambler during his game to show him the remains of the ship's [[neutrino hybridizer]] and to inform him that, just as the pair had suspected earlier, the explosion had been caused by a bomb. After receiving the news, Calrissian retired for the night to his [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] room.<ref name="LCFO" />

On Calrissian's fourth night on Oseon 6845, his sabacc game with Lob Doluff and his associates, which took place at a different venue each evening, was held at the [[Oseon Administration|Administrator Senior's]] [[Doluff's estate|estate]]. While the game was taking place, a message was sent to Doluff that relayed a false alarm claiming that the ''Millennium Falcon'', docked at Oseon 6845's [[north polar spaceport]], had caught [[Fire/Legends|fire]]. Believing the alarm to be genuine, Calrissian traveled to the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], only to discover that there was, in fact, no fire aboard his ship.<ref name="LCFO" />

===A turn of fate===
{{Quote|Well, sir, the false alarm seems to have been intended as a trap. Someone ambushed me as I was preparing to return to the sabacc game—a stranger. I'm rather afraid I've killed him, Administrator Senior.|Lando Calrissian, to Lob Doluff|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
[[File:VuffiNew.png|thumb|right|300px|The Renatasian Confederation targeted Vuffi Raa (pictured) on Oseon 6845.]]
As the gambler prepared to return to his game, he was ambushed in one of the spaceport's [[Service corridor 17-W|service corridors]] by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Kenow]], a member of<ref name="LCFO" /> the [[Renatasian Confederation]]<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref>—a [[Renatasian|group]] devoted to exacting vengeance upon Vuffi Raa, whom it wrongfully held responsible for the [[Battle of Renatasia|subjugation]] of the [[Renatasia system/Legends|Renatasia system]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="LCFO" /> Kenow had been tasked with intercepting Raa on Oseon 6845.<ref name="CSWE Kenow">{{CSWECite|II|212|Kenow, Colonel}}</ref> Acting in self-defense, Calrissian [[Death/Legends|killed]] Kenow with his [[Stingbeam/Legends|stingbeam]] [[Blaster pistol/Legends|pistol]].<ref name="LCFO" />

After Calrissian informed Doluff of the incident, [[Oseon Peacekeeper]] Captain [[Bassi Vobah]]—with whom Calrissian had played sabacc at Doluff's estate on that same night—arrived at the spaceport. The [[Police officer/Legends|officer]] examined Kenow's body and placed the gambler under arrest, with Vuffi Raa subsequently being outfitted with a [[Restraining bolt/Legends|restraining bolt]] aboard the ''Millennium Falcon'' by the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]].<ref name="LCFO" />

===A sinister plot===
{{Quote|I and I alone arranged for that decadent leviathan to be harrassed by the government, then had him killed and took his place. All so I would be here when you arrived.|Rokur Gepta, on using Bohhuah Mutdah to entrap Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
[[File:RokurGeptaCloseup-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|300px|Rokur Gepta spent time meditating on Oseon 6845.]]
A few hours after Calrissian's arrest, while the gambler was still [[Prisoner/Legends|incarcerated]] in a local [[Prison/Legends|prison]] cell and Captain Vobah was writing up a report on the spaceport killing, the Flamewind phenomenon commenced in the Oseon system. By that [[Time/Legends|time]], Rokur Gepta had arrived at Oseon 6845 on his personal [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], the ''[[Wennis]]'', in pursuit of the ''Millennium Falcon''. Gepta arranged for Imperial authorities to order the arrest of [[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah]], a retired trillionaire industrialist and the wealthiest individual in the Oseon system, though making it appear as if Mutdah's business rivals were having him arrested to plot his downfall.<ref name="LCFO" />

To that end, the Imperial undercover [[Spice/Legends|narcotics]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] [[Waywa Fybot]] was dispatched to the Oseon system to make the arrest. As an added insurance, Doluff was pressured to ensure that the arrest went through. Gepta himself spent the time that followed Calrissian's arrest [[Meditation/Legends|meditating]] in an artificial [[Cave/Legends|cavern]] close to the asteroid's core, while the ''Wennis'' was stationed some distance away with its crew performing a series of drill exercises. Also stationed at that time in the shadow of Oseon 6845, several thousand kilometers from the asteroid, was the [[Zero Squadron|squadron]] of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] making up the Renatasian Confederation.<ref name="LCFO" />

===The deal===
{{Quote|As I said, the punishment as prescribed by law is exposure to the heat, cold, and vacuum of interplanetary space. There is, however, no provision for the precise method to be employed, and I am moved, my boy, to suggest a means by which the law may be obeyed and yet spare you from the unpleasantness such an experience ordinarily brings.|Lob Doluff, to Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
[[File:MillenniumFalcon-FlamewindOfOseon.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Lando Calrissian was forced to brave the Flamewind phenomenon in his freighter, the ''Millennium Falcon''.]]
Doluff eventually ordered Calrissian to be brought to his [[Administrator Senior's office|office]], where Fybot and Vobah were also present. The Administrator Senior explained to the gambler that he was charged with carrying a lethal weapon, which was a capital offense in the Oseon system. However, the traditional Oseoni sentence, death by [[Spacing/Legends|exposure]] to the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] of interplanetary space, would be exchanged for Calrissian being forced to participate in a sting operation.<ref name="LCFO" />

The gambler was to [[Transport/Legends|transport]] Fybot and Vobah to [[Oseon 5792]], Mutdah's private asteroid in the [[Fifth Belt]] of the system—a trip made extremely hazardous due to the Flamewind interfering with both living beings and the equipment of starships. At his destination, Calrissian would pose as a [[Courier/Legends|courier]] delivering Mutdah his scheduled supply of the illegal [[lesai]] drug, allowing the two law enforcement [[Officer/Legends|officers]] to arrest the trillionaire. In addition, Doluff, correctly suspecting Fybot of having been secretly ordered to kill Mutdah, explicitly ordered the agent to bring the industrialist back to Oseon 6845 to stand trial.<ref name="LCFO" />

===Departing Oseon 6845===
{{Quote|This is Captain Calrissian speaking. Hope you two are thoroughly uncomfortable over there. We're off the ground and headed toward the Fifth Belt.|Lando Calrissian, to Waywa Fybot and Bassi Vobah|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
[[File:LandoGepta.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Lando Calrissian left Oseon 6845 for what was, unbeknownst to him, a trap designed by Rokur Gepta.]]
The following day, as part of the preparations for leaving for Oseon 5792, Calrissian and Vuffi Raa outfitted the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} passenger lounge with a seating rack designed to accommodate Fybot's<ref name="LCFO" /> [[Quor'sav/Legends|Quor'sav]]<ref name="TERC" /> physiology. At the same time, the [[Unidentified first officer (Wennis)|first officer]] of the ''Wennis'' surreptitiously met Fybot near a [[power transformer]] in the depths of Oseon 6845. The [[Executive officer/Legends|officer]] reminded Fybot of his secret orders in light of Doluff's interference with the intentions of their superiors and then departed for a meeting with Gepta in the asteroid's core.<ref name="LCFO" />

Shortly thereafter, with both Fybot and Vobah in the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} lounge and Doluff observing the progress of the ship's departure via [[Data-link/Legends|datalink]] in his office, Calrissian's freighter left Oseon 6845. Alerted by a [[Missile/Legends|missile]] sent as a signal by the first officer of the ''Wennis'', who was colluding with the Renatasian Confederation, the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] also departed the asteroid in pursuit of Calrissian's vessel. Eventually, Gepta, too, left Oseon 6845 aboard his [[Rokur Gepta's starfighter|personal starfighter]], leaving his cruiser behind and instructing it to rendezvous with him in the [[Tund system/Legends|Tund system]] after the Flamewind had passed. Gepta traveled to Oseon 5792, where he had Mutdah murdered and then assumed his appearance in order to entrap Calrissian.<ref name="LCFO" />

Around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="UnderworldDate">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}} is set a few [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the several months immediately following which correspond to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Maximillian Seerdon]] of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] prepared a report on various elements of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] underworld that posed a threat to the Empire's stability. In the report, Seerdon included an entry on Calrissian, which listed several of the gambler's exploits and adventures, including his arrest on Oseon 6845.<ref name="Underworld" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''[…]'' I assure you that no one you played with, excepting Miss Vobah here, who was appropriately subsidized, will miss so much as a micro of your winnings. We are a wealthy people.|Lob Doluff, to Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
The entire Oseon system was inhabited by individuals of notable wealth. With that in mind, most of the establishments on Oseon 6845 were geared toward catering to affluent locals as well as attracting tourists from at least a million other systems of the wider galaxy. It was sometimes said that some among the pedestrians walking along the throughway known as the [[Esplanade]] were the wealthiest individuals in the [[Universe/Legends|known universe]].<ref name="LCFO" />

Among the patrons of Oseon 6845's locales were [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]–speaking Humans as well as members of a variety of other [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] and [[Alien/Legends|nonhuman]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Droids were also ubiquitous, being employed in both public areas and on private property.<ref name="LCFO" />

[[File:Sabacc_naboo.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Gambling on games such as sabacc was popular on Oseon 6845.]]
Entertainment venues on Oseon 6845, such as Hotel Drofo, were frequented by [[Noble/Legends|nobility]], active and retired businessbeings, merchants, freight haulers, military officers, [[Media/Legends|media]] stars, and even criminals, including [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and smugglers. High-stakes [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] was a popular pastime for the affluent beings on Oseon 6845. For approximately three [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] every [[Standard year/Legends|year]], all interasteroidal travel was forbidden during the Flamewind phenomenon. During that period, the Oseon locals and tourists enjoyed themselves at numerous public and private parties.<ref name="LCFO" />

The government of the Oseon system, the Oseon Administration, was based on Oseon 6845. It was headed by the Administrator Senior and employed the Oseon Peacekeepers as a local police force. The Administration enforced the laws of the star system, such as the one forbidding the carrying of a lethal weapon, for which the sentence upon conviction was death by exposure to the vacuum of space.<ref name="LCFO" />

==Locations==
===Underground===
{{Quote|Excuse me, gentlebeing. I have a message for one of the players inside.|Vuffi Raa, to a Hotel Drofo bouncer|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
Oseon 6845 was in its entirety artificially honeycombed. One such cavity was an artificial cavern deep below the asteroid's surface. The sorcerer Rokur Gepta at one point spent time in the zero-gravity cavern.<ref name="LCFO" />

[[File:Drofo.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Drofo the Third was the owner of Hotel Drofo on Oseon 6845.]]
Somewhat closer to the surface of Oseon 6845, yet still deep underground, air, [[Water/Legends|water]], and other substances vital to living beings were transported from [[Nuclear fission|fission]]-powered machines to the various inhabited areas of the asteroid via pipes that were the size of a man in diameter. Also located in the area was a power transformer that was starship-sized and emitted a powerful humming sound. The air in the area containing the machinery was cold despite the equipment being in full operation. Only droids occasionally visited the locality while performing routine tasks, although shortly before leaving the asteroid, Wayva Fybot clandestinely met there with the first officer of the cruiser ''Wennis''.<ref name="LCFO" />

The interior of Oseon 6845 was filled with luxurious hotels, night entertainment venues, and palace-like residences. One such location was the exclusive Hotel Drofo,<ref name="LCFO" /> owned by the [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] [[Drofo III|Drofo the Third]]. Drofo kept an [[891c Akorec-class strike cruiser|Akorec strike cruiser]] as protection against potential [[Thief/Legends|thieves]].<ref name="Gamer">{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}}</ref> Since Oseon 6845's surface area was both limited<ref name="LCFO" /> and entirely covered by nightclubs and resorts,<ref name="Guide">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> construction on its surface was costly as opposed to building into the asteroid's interior. As a result, while the entrance to Hotel Drofo was located on Oseon 6845's surface, the hotel proper was located throughout several floors below the ground, and the establishment's [[Grand Lobby]] was located on the bottom floor. Small, luxurious gambling salons lay next to the lobby. When Lando Calrissian visited Oseon 6845, he stayed in a room at Hotel Drofo that featured a [[Sleeper (furniture)|bed]] that could turn itself down.<ref name="LCFO" />

===Equatorial locations===
{{Quote|Have you had anything to eat?''"<br />"''Best food I've ever had in jail.|Lob Doluff and Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
The surface entrance of Hotel Drofo opened out onto the Esplanade. A kilometers-long, domed pedestrian thoroughfare<ref name="LCFO" /> that stretched around Oseon 6845's equator<ref name="Gamer" /> and was constructed by Lob Doluff, the Administrator Senior of the Oseon system,<ref name="CSWE Doluff">{{CSWECite|I|196|Doluff, Lob}}</ref> it was lined with [[Tree/Legends|trees]], sculptures, and elegant stores that catered to the wealthy inhabitants and visitors of the asteroid. One of the Esplanade's landscape elements, which were intentionally formed to be full of surprises, was a decorative pond inhabited by a species of [[Fish/Legends|fish]] as well as a small, many-[[Limb/Legends|jointed]] [[Unidentified creature (Esplanade)|creature]].<ref name="LCFO" />

[[File:StarcaveLando.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Lando Calrissian was kept in a jail on Oseon 6845 following an arrest for carrying a lethal weapon.]]
The Esplanade constituted the center of a [[Unidentified city (Oseon 6845)|small city]] located on Oseon 6845's equator. A prison suite decorated in cheery [[Plastic/Legends|plastic]] colors was situated within a few kilometers of the [[City/Legends|city]]. It was outfitted with a barred-and-shielded skylight, a separate [[Refresher/Legends|bathroom]] with a large [[Mirror/Legends|mirror]] in it, and a [[Speaker/Legends|speaker]] in the ceiling through which tasteless [[Music/Legends|music]] was played. Lando Calrissian was incarcerated in the cell during his visit to the asteroid. During his stay there, the gambler was given a set of pajamas with a number printed on them in six [[Language/Legends|languages]]. Calrissian later stated to Oseon 6845's Administrator Senior Doluff that he was given the best [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] he had ever had while imprisoned.<ref name="LCFO" />

Elsewhere on Oseon 6845's equator, Doluff maintained his personal estate. It was located slightly further away from the small equatorial city than the prison suite in which Calrissian was held. In essence both a [[Garden/Legends|garden]] and a greenhouse, the estate was a relatively large dome on the asteroid's surface. It was filled with a thick [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] of tropical [[Plant/Legends|plants]], and the air in it was moist. The dome featured a tiled walkway in its center, with a sabacc table placed on it and a fountain located nearby. In a semitropical area of the estate, a tree stump had a hidden compartment with a [[Comlink/Legends|communicator]] in it. Doluff [[Collector/Legends|collected]] [[Flower/Legends|flowering]] plants, and a special section of his estate, filled with half a [[Hectare/Legends|hectare]] of snow and ice, featured minuscule [[snow-flower]]s from a hundred different star systems.<ref name="LCFO" />

===Other locations===
{{Quote|This is the Administrator Senior. Have Captain Calrissian brought to my office in an hour.|Lob Doluff, via communicator|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
[[File:Planet11-SWR.png|thumb|left|300px|Administrator Senior Lob Doluff kept a lizard from Zebitrope IV (pictured) in his office on Oseon 6845.]]
The Administrator Senior's office was essentially a miniature version of Lob Doluff's estate. It was a room featuring a pair of alcoves and a floor-to-ceiling [[Window/Legends|window]]. The office was filled with flora, with a multitude of hanging, potted, aquatic, and flying plants scattered about the room, including a species of [[Fern (plant)/Legends|fern]] and a [[Unidentified flying plant|winged plant]]. Doluff had a pair of [[Secretary/Legends|secretaries]] in an anteroom of the office. There was no desk in the office itself, but several chairs were present. A datalink was hidden in one the alcoves, while the other recess featured a [[Terrarium/Legends|terrarium]]. The latter housed a collection of unusual [[Unidentified spongy growth|spongy growths]] and a [[Unidentified lizard (Zebitrope IV)|lizard]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the planet [[Zebitrope IV]], which Doluff used to obtain the lesai drug for personal use.<ref name="LCFO" />

Due to the landing of starships being relatively easier on the north pole of Oseon 6845, a small spaceport had been established there. Leveled out of the rock of a huge natural crater many kilometers in diameter, the port consisted of a landing field positioned over a labyrinth of service corridors and passageways. Various pleasure [[Yacht/Legends|yachts]], company starships, and even a [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battle cruiser]] were parked at the spaceport. During Lando Calrissian's visit on the asteroid, his freighter, the ''Millennium Falcon'', was docked above service corridor 17-W, with both the vessel and its owner's names being indicated in six languages at the site. A pneumatic tubeway traveling along a straight line beneath Oseon 6845's surface connected the spaceport to Doluff's estate.<ref name="LCFO" /> Entrance and egress portals for [[Tube car/Legends|tube cars]] and various starships also perforated the entire asteroid.<ref name="Gamer" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Flamewind_of_Oseon_Cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Oseon 6845 was introduced in ''Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon''.]]
Oseon 6845 was introduced in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', the [[1983]] second entry in [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels by [[L. Neil Smith]]. On the original cover of the novel, an illustration by [[William Schmidt]] depicted several buildings on Oseon 6845.<ref name="LCFO" /> "[[Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy]]," an article written by [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] and published in [[Star Wars Insider 89|''Star Wars Insider'' 89]]<ref name="Underworld" /> by [[July 28]], [[2006]],<ref name="Insider89date">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20060728.html|text=Explore the Underworld in ''Star Wars Insider'' #89}}</ref> introduced a shortened name for the asteroid.<ref name="Underworld" />

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the Oseon system, and therefore Oseon 6845, in the [[Zebitrope sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the Oseon system was situated in grid square T-8 as part of the Centrality.<ref name="TEA" />
